title: Filter Controls
text:
Filter controls let users explore VVN data by region, year, and topic. Every filter must have a clear label, a tooltip, and a default value. Always pair Apply Filters with a Reset button.
Use vvn_filter(), vvn_slider(), and vvn_button() from the vvnthemes library to build the sidebar panel.

For filter component functions and code examples, see the Interactive Components Library →

title: Filter Controls
text:
Filter controls let users explore VVN data by region, year, and topic. Every filter must have a clear label, a tooltip, and a default value. Always pair Apply Filters with a Reset button.
Use vvn_filter(), vvn_slider(), and vvn_button() from the vvnthemes library to build the sidebar panel.

For filter component functions and code examples, see the Interactive Components Library →

title: Filter Controls
text:
How to add filters



1


Add the sidebar panelUse create_vvn_dashboard() to scaffold the sidebar. The sidebar is 260 px wide with a #F7F7F7 background. The panel header shows “Filters” in white bold on a VT Maroon bar.







2


Add dropdowns and slidersCall vvn_filter() for each dropdown and vvn_slider() for each range input. Set a default value for every filter — never leave a filter blank on first load.







3


Write a tooltip for each filterEvery filter requires a ⓘ tooltip. The tooltip must explain what the filter controls, what the options mean, and when the data was last updated.







4


Add Apply and Reset buttonsUse vvn_button() for both buttons. Apply Filters is full-width VT Maroon. Reset is outline style, directly below Apply. Never omit the Reset button.

title: Filter Controls
text:
Required elements
Every VVN filter panel must include all four elements.









#
Element
Standard
Library function




1
Filter header
Full-width VT Maroon #861F41. “Filters” in white bold. Include a funnel icon and a “Clear All” reset link.
create_vvn_dashboard()


2
Dropdown or slider
Bold label above each input. Set a clear default value. Group related filters under a subheading.
vvn_filter(), vvn_slider()


3
Info tooltip (ⓘ)
Required on every filter. Explains what it controls, available options, and data vintage.
vvn_filter()


4
Apply + Reset buttons
Apply: VT Maroon #861F41, full width. Reset: outline style, placed directly below Apply. Both required.
vvn_button()

title: Filter Controls
text:
Design standards
Sidebar layout

Width: 260 px fixed
Background: #F7F7F7
Header bar: VT Maroon #861F41, “Filters” label in white bold

Labels

Every filter must have a bold label placed above the input
Label text: sentence case, charcoal #3D3D3D
Never use placeholder text as a substitute for a label

Tooltips

Every filter must have a ⓘ icon tooltip
Tooltip must state: what the filter controls, what each option means, and when data was last updated
Tooltip text: 1–3 sentences; avoid jargon

Apply and Reset buttons

Apply Filters: VT Maroon fill, white text, full width of the sidebar
Reset: outline style (maroon border, transparent fill), placed immediately below Apply
Never omit the Reset button — users must be able to return to the default view
Disable Apply until at least one filter has changed from its default

Default values

Every filter must have a sensible default that shows data on first load
Never require the user to set a filter before the dashboard will display



For step-by-step R code and filter examples, see the Interactive Components Library →

title: VVN Insights
text:
VVN Insights is a Quarto HTML document that tells one data story. It combines a written narrative with charts, an interactive county map, and a summary table — all styled to the VVN design standard.
Use it when you want readers to follow a single argument from beginning to end. For interactive data exploration with filters, use Interactive Data Visualizations instead.

title: VVN Insights
section: How to create a VVN Insight
text:
How to create a VVN Insight



1


Scaffold the projectRun one command in R. The library creates the full folder, styles, and placeholder content — the document renders immediately with no setup.

VVN Insight Library →









2


Load your dataReplace the sample data at the top of index.qmd with your own CSV. Your data frame needs at minimum: fips (character), county (character), year (integer), and value (numeric).







3


Write the narrativeReplace every [placeholder] with your content. Lead with the key finding. Reference every figure in the text before it appears (“Figure 1 shows…”). Keep paragraphs to 3–4 sentences.







4


Build figures to VVN standardsEvery chart and map needs a title, subtitle, and source line. Use theme_vvn(), VVN palettes, and vvn_source(). See Charts and Maps for full rules.







5


Render and publishRun quarto render index.qmd. Make sure embed-resources: true is set in the YAML so the output is a single self-contained HTML file. Check the Publishing Checklist before sharing.

title: VVN Insights
text:
Required elements
Every VVN Insight must include all seven elements. Use the vvnthemes library functions to build each one.









#
Element
Standard
Library / tool




1
Hero banner
Full-width VT Maroon #861F41. “VVN INSIGHTS” label in small caps; title in white bold, headline case; one-sentence description below.
create_vvn_story()


2
Headline
White, headline case, bold. States topic and geographic scope in one line.
create_vvn_story()


3
Date & source tag
Publication date + primary data source below the headline. Include dataset name and vintage year.
create_vvn_story()


4
Choropleth map
County-level interactive Leaflet map. maroon_seq palette. Tooltip: county name + value. Referenced in text before it appears.
vvn_map_style()


5
KPI callouts
Key statistics highlighted in callout boxes. Use sparingly — only the most important 2–3 numbers.
vvn_kpi_card()


6
Narrative sections
Short paragraphs (3–4 sentences). Section headings in sentence case. Sections: Overview, Background, Data & Methods, Findings, Discussion, Conclusion.
Quarto markdown


7
Source lines
Required on every figure. Format: “Source: [Agency, Dataset, Year].”
vvn_source()

title: VVN Insights
text:
Design standards
Hero banner

Background: VT Maroon #861F41
“VVN INSIGHTS” label: small caps, 0.75 rem, rgba(255,255,255,0.75)
Title: white, bold, headline case, max 2 lines
Description: white, sentence case, 1 rem

Narrative

Lead with the key finding — state the main result in the first paragraph
Every figure must be referenced in the text before it appears: “Figure 1 shows that …”
Paragraphs: 3–4 sentences maximum
Section headings: sentence case (## level)
Include sections for: Overview · Background · Data & Methods · Findings · Discussion · Conclusion

Figures
Follow all Chart Standards and Map Standards. Source lines are required on every figure without exception.
KPI callouts

Use only for the 2–3 most important statistics
Orange accent (#E5751F) for emphasis
Never use KPI callouts in place of a proper explanation in the narrative



For the full annotated scaffold with all sections pre-built, see the VVN Insight Library →

title: Maps
text:
VVN maps visualize geographic patterns at the Virginia county level. All maps use consistent palettes, clear legends, and informative tooltips. Every map must be referenced in the text before it appears.
Use an interactive Leaflet map in dashboards (users hover for values). Use a static ggplot map in VVN Insights (rendered to PNG for the final document).

For R functions, palette options, and map implementation, see the Map Library →

title: Maps
text:
VVN maps visualize geographic patterns at the Virginia county level. All maps use consistent palettes, clear legends, and informative tooltips. Every map must be referenced in the text before it appears.
Use an interactive Leaflet map in dashboards (users hover for values). Use a static ggplot map in VVN Insights (rendered to PNG for the final document).

For R functions, palette options, and map implementation, see the Map Library →

title: Maps
text:
Required elements









#
Element
Standard
Library function




1
Title
Headline case. Names the variable and geography. Define any abbreviations.
labs(title = ...) + theme_vvn_map()


2
Subtitle
Sentence case. States data source and vintage year. Example: “2020 ACS 5-Year Estimates.”
labs(subtitle = ...)


3
Legend
Inside the map panel. Include variable name and units in the label. Use the VVN palette that matches the data type.
scale_fill_vvn_c()


4
Tooltip
Interactive maps only. Shows county name, metric value, and unit on hover. Format values consistently with the legend.
vvn_map_style()


5
Source line
Required on every map. Format: “Source: [Agency, Dataset, Year].” Add Notes for abbreviations and suppressed data.
vvn_source()


6
Basemap
Muted light gray. WGS84 (CRS 4326) for Leaflet; Albers Equal-Area (CRS 5070) for static ggplot maps.
vvn_map_style() / sf


7
Color palette
Sequential: maroon_seq or orange_seq. Diverging: diverging. Categorical: main or accessible.
vvn_palette(), scale_fill_vvn_c()

title: Maps
text:
Design standards
Palette selection



Data type
Palette




Sequential (low → high)
maroon_seq (default) or orange_seq


Diverging (negative ↔︎ positive, change)
diverging


Categorical (unordered groups)
main or accessible



Never use a rainbow or non-VVN palette. Color must encode one variable only.
County boundaries

Border: thin white, 0.5 px
Suppressed or missing counties: light gray fill with a note in the source line

Tooltip (interactive maps)

County name: bold
Value: formatted with units (e.g., “72.4%”, “$48,200”)
Never display raw unformatted numbers

Static maps (VVN Insights)

CRS: Albers Equal-Area (EPSG 5070) — preserves county area proportions
Export at 300 dpi with vvn_save()
Do not crop the legend out of the frame



For step-by-step R code and map examples, see the Map Library →

title: VVN Style Guide
text:
Visualizing Virginia Numbers · Virginia Tech



Design standards for every dashboard and report published by the Visualizing Virginia Numbers initiative.

title: VVN Style Guide
text:
What are you building?
VVN produces two types of products. Each has its own page in this guide.


Dashboard
An interactive R Shiny application — filters, KPI cards, charts, a county map, and a data table. Built for exploration.
Dashboard Standards →


VVN Insights
A Quarto HTML document — hero banner, narrative, charts, a map, and a summary table. Built for reading.
Insights Standards →

title: VVN Style Guide
section: How to build a VVN product
text:
How to build a VVN product



1


Choose your product typeUse a Dashboard when users need to filter and explore data interactively. Use VVN Insights when you are telling a single data story for reading.Dashboard →VVN Insights →







2


Scaffold with the vvnthemes libraryOne R command creates the full project — all files, folders, styles, and placeholder content already in place. No setup required.Dashboard Library →VVN Insight Library →







3


Load and prepare your dataStore raw files in data/. All cleaning and derived variables belong in the data prep script — not in chart code. Document every source.







4


Build figures following VVN standardsEvery chart, map, and table requires a title (headline case), subtitle (sentence case), and source line. Apply theme_vvn() and VVN palettes — never use off-palette colors.Charts →Maps →Tables →







5


Write the narrativeLead with the key finding. Reference every figure in the text before it appears. Keep paragraphs to 3–4 sentences. Include background, methods, and limitations.







6


PublishDashboard: deploy with rsconnect::deployApp()  ·  VVN Insight: render with quarto render index.qmd and embed-resources: true.Publishing Checklist →

title: VVN Style Guide
text:
Design standards
Use the sidebar to find standards for each component. Every figure in both products must follow these rules.



Standard
Applies to




Brand — colors, typography, logo
All products


Charts — titles, axes, palettes, source lines
Dashboards + Insights


Maps — choropleth, legend, basemap
Dashboards + Insights


Tables — headers, alignment, source notes
Dashboards + Insights


Filter Controls — labels, tooltips, Apply/Reset
Dashboards only

title: Charts
text:
VVN charts use a white background, VT Maroon titles, and VVN palettes. Every chart must be self-explanatory — a reader should understand the key message without reading surrounding text.

For R functions, code examples, and implementation, see the Chart Library →

title: Charts
text:
VVN charts use a white background, VT Maroon titles, and VVN palettes. Every chart must be self-explanatory — a reader should understand the key message without reading surrounding text.

For R functions, code examples, and implementation, see the Chart Library →

title: Charts
text:
Required elements
Every VVN chart must include all of the following elements.









#
Element
Standard
Library function




1
Title
Headline case, active language. VT Maroon #861F41, bold, one line. State units once only — in the subtitle or y-axis, never both.
labs(title = ...) + theme_vvn()


2
Subtitle
Sentence case. Qualifies the title with time period, geography, units, or frequency. Never headline case.
labs(subtitle = ...)


3
Axis titles & labels
Sentence case. First letter and proper nouns capitalized only. Abbreviations defined in Notes below the chart.
theme_vvn()


4
Legend
Place at top of chart, between title and plot area. Mirror data order. Use direct labels on the chart and omit the legend whenever possible.
theme_vvn()


5
Data labels
Place close to the data point. Use sparingly — highlight important or surprising values only, not every data point.
geom_text() / geom_label()


6
Source line
Required on every chart. Format: “Source: [Author, Title, Date].” Small gray text at bottom left.
vvn_source()


7
Notes
Below source line. Define abbreviations, methodology, and significance levels. Superscript letters link notes to specific data points.
vvn_note()

title: Charts
text:
Design standards
Colors

Use only VVN palettes: main, brand, maroon_seq, orange_seq, diverging, or accessible
Never use off-palette colors, gradients, or transparency for data encoding
Apply the palette with scale_color_vvn() or scale_fill_vvn()

Typography

Title: VT Maroon #861F41, bold, headline case
Subtitle: charcoal #3D3D3D, sentence case
Axis labels: charcoal, sentence case, same font size as body text
Source and notes: gray #AAAAAA, 0.75 rem

Chart types







Use this
When




Horizontal bar
Comparing categories with long labels


Vertical bar
Comparing categories with short labels or time periods


Line
Showing change over time for one or more series


Scatter
Showing the relationship between two continuous variables


Small multiples
Comparing the same chart across groups or regions



Saving
Export at 300 dpi using vvn_save(). Default dimensions: 8 × 5 inches.


For step-by-step R code and chart examples, see the Chart Library →

title: Interactive Data Visualizations
text:
Interactive Data Visualizations is a VVN dashboard — an interactive R Shiny web application. It lets users filter and explore VVN data on their own — choosing regions, years, and topics while charts, KPI cards, a county map, and a data table all update in real time.
Use it when users need to explore data. For a fixed narrative with a single finding, use a VVN Insights report instead.

title: Interactive Data Visualizations
text:
How to create a dashboard



1


Scaffold the projectRun one command in R. The library creates the full folder structure, all required files, VVN styles, and placeholder content — the dashboard runs immediately with no setup.

Dashboard Library →









2


Load your data in R/data_prep.RReplace the sample data with your own CSV or database query. Your data frame needs at minimum: region (character), year (integer), and value (numeric). All charts and cards update automatically.







3


Build figures to VVN standardsEvery chart and map needs a title, subtitle, and source line. Use theme_vvn() and VVN palettes. See Charts and Maps for full rules.







4


Configure filtersEvery filter needs a bold label, a tooltip, and a default value. Always pair Apply Filters with a Reset button. See Filter Controls.







5


DeployRun rsconnect::deployApp() from the project root. Check the Publishing Checklist before deploying.

title: Interactive Data Visualizations
text:
Required elements
Every VVN Dashboard must include all six elements. Use the vvnthemes library functions to build each one.









#
Element
Standard
Library function




1
Navbar
Full-width VT Maroon #861F41. Project name in white bold. Orange bottom border (3 px).
create_vvn_dashboard()


2
KPI Cards
Max 3 per row. Large value, short label, optional trend. One accent color per card — maroon, orange, or navy.
vvn_kpi_card()


3
Sidebar Filters
260 px wide, #F7F7F7 background. Bold label + tooltip on every filter. Apply + Reset buttons required.
vvn_filter(), vvn_slider(), vvn_button()


4
Choropleth Map
County-level Leaflet map. maroon_seq palette. Tooltip with county name and value. Legend inside panel.
vvn_map_style()


5
Charts
White background, VT Maroon titles, VVN palettes, source line in every caption.
theme_vvn(), vvn_source()


6
Footer
White text on VT Maroon background. Shows “Visualizing Virginia Numbers · Virginia Tech”.
create_vvn_dashboard()

title: Interactive Data Visualizations
text:
Design standards
Navbar

Background: VT Maroon #861F41 · Bottom border: VT Orange #E5751F, 3 px
Project name: white, bold, sentence case
Tab links: rgba(255,255,255,0.9) — never pure white

KPI Cards

Left border accent: maroon, orange, or navy — one color per card, never mixed in the same row
Value: 2 rem, bold · Label: 0.82 rem, charcoal, sentence case
Maximum three cards per row

Sidebar Filters

Width: 260 px fixed · Background: #F7F7F7
Every filter must have: a bold label above, a tooltip (ⓘ) explaining what it controls, and a clear default
Apply Filters button: VT Maroon, full width · Reset button: outline style, directly below Apply

Map

Palette: maroon_seq for sequential data, diverging for change or comparison
County borders: thin white (0.5 px) · Tooltip: county name + formatted value + unit
Never use a non-VVN palette

Charts
Follow all Chart Standards. Source lines are required on every chart, even inside a dashboard.
Footer

Background: VT Maroon #861F41 · Text: white, 0.8 rem
Content: “Visualizing Virginia Numbers · Virginia Tech”



For the full annotated scaffold and deployment guide, see the Dashboard Library →

title: Publishing Checklist
text:
ImportantBefore you start



Search your file for [ — if any placeholder text remains, stop and fill it in first. Never publish with unfilled brackets.

title: Publishing Checklist
text:
Dashboard

See Dashboard Library → and Interactive Components →

Complete all items before running rsconnect::deployApp().

Layout & navigation

Navbar background: VT Maroon #861F41 · white project name, bold · orange bottom border 3 px
Exactly 3 KPI cards per row — one card per key metric, colors maroon / orange / navy
Each KPI card has: metric value · short label · optional trend indicator (up / down / flat) with comparison period
Sidebar: 260 px wide · background #F7F7F7
Every filter has a tooltip (ⓘ icon) explaining what it controls and when data was last updated
Apply Filters button: maroon #861F41, full width · Reset button directly below (outline style)
Page footer: white text on maroon · “Visualizing Virginia Numbers · Virginia Tech”



Tab structure

Tab 1 — Overview: sidebar filters + 3 KPI cards + trend line chart + comparison bar chart + distribution chart
Tab 2 — Map: Leaflet choropleth · Virginia county-level · WGS84 (CRS 4326) · maroon_seq or diverging palette · legend inside panel · tooltip shows county name + value + range on hover
Tab 3 — Data: paginated, searchable, sortable DT table · responds to sidebar filters · CSV download button · Excel download button · no raw IDs or processing artifacts
Tab 4 — About: 1–2 paragraph project description + full data source citations + methods note + contact email



All figures inside the dashboard

Every chart: theme_vvn() applied · title (headline case) · subtitle (sentence case) · source line in caption
Only VVN palette colors — no off-palette colors anywhere
Map data normalized — rate or percentage, never raw counts



Publish

No [PLACEHOLDER] text remains
rsconnect::deployApp() runs without errors

title: Publishing Checklist
text:
VVN Insights

See VVN Insight Library →

Complete all items before running quarto render index.qmd.

Document sections (required in order)

1 — Hero banner: #861F41 background · “VVN INSIGHTS” label in small caps · title white bold (headline case) · one-sentence description · publication date + primary data source below headline
2 — Key Finding: 1–2 sentences immediately after the hero · the single most important result · key number bolded — not buried in the conclusion
3 — Background: why this topic matters for Virginia · specific question addressed · intended audience named
4 — Data & Methods: source table (Source / Geography / Period / Variable) + callout noting caveats or comparability issues
5 — Findings: at least 2 charts · 1 map (interactive or static) · 1 summary table · organized into subsections by theme or geographic pattern
6 — Discussion: interpretation (what findings mean, not just what they show) + prior research context + equity implications + limitations
7 — Conclusion: 2–3 sentence takeaway + policy implication or future direction + contact information + VVN and Virginia Tech attribution



Figures in the Insight

Every figure referenced in the narrative before it appears — “Figure 1 shows…”
Every chart: title (headline case) · subtitle (sentence case) · source line
Every map: normalized data · title · legend · source line
Every table: maroon header · alternating rows · em dash — for missing values · source line
KPI callout boxes used only for the most important statistics (orange accent)



Publish

embed-resources: true set in the document YAML
No [PLACEHOLDER] text remains
quarto render index.qmd completes with 0 errors · 0 warnings

title: Publishing Checklist
text:
All Figures
These rules apply to every chart, map, and table in both product types.

Required elements — every figure

Title — headline case (capitalize all major words)
Subtitle — sentence case · states geography and/or year
Source line — “Source: [Agency, Dataset, Year].” — no exceptions
Unit stated once only — subtitle or axis label, never both



Charts

See Chart Library →


theme_vvn() applied to all ggplot2 figures
Only VVN palette colors: Maroon #861F41 · Orange #E5751F · Navy #1B5299
Legend title present if the chart shows multiple series
Notes appear below the source line when abbreviations or methodology need explanation
Significance stars use symbols in order: *, †, ‡, § — never use asterisks for general notes



Maps

See Map Library →


Data normalized — rate or percentage, never raw counts on a choropleth
Sequential data: maroon_seq (primary) or orange_seq (secondary)
Above/below-average data: diverging palette
Categorical / discrete groups: main or accessible palette
Interactive (Leaflet): WGS84 CRS 4326 · tooltip shows county name + value + range
Static (ggplot2 + sf): Albers Equal-Area CRS 5070



Tables

See Table Library →


Maroon #861F41 header row, white bold text
Column headings: sentence case · spanner headings: headline case
Alternating light gray #F7F7F7 body rows · no internal borders by default
Numbers decimal-aligned (bottom right) · text left-aligned (bottom left)
Em dash — for all missing values — never leave a cell blank
Abbreviations defined below the table (e.g., “FPL = federal poverty level”)
Significance noted with superscript letters linked to footnotes

title: Brand
text: For palette functions and color utilities in R, see the vvnthemes R Library →

title: Brand
text:
Color Palette

Brand Colors






VT Maroon #861F41 Primary — navbars, titles, chart fill







VT Orange #E5751F Secondary — highlights, buttons, callouts







Navy #1B5299 Tertiary — chart series, accent







Charcoal #3D3D3D Body text, axis labels







Gray #AAAAAA Captions, secondary text







Light Gray #F7F7F7 Backgrounds, sidebar, table rows



The three colors used in charts and multi-series visualizations are Maroon, Orange, and Navy — access them via scale_fill_vvn("brand").



Palettes








Palette
Type
Use




brand
Categorical
3-group comparisons — maroon, orange, navy


main
Categorical
General purpose — up to 10 categories


accessible
Categorical
Colorblind-safe — use when accessibility is critical


maroon_seq
Sequential
Primary continuous metric — choropleth maps


orange_seq
Sequential
Secondary continuous metric


navy_seq
Sequential
Tertiary continuous metric


diverging
Diverging
Data with a meaningful midpoint (above/below average)









WarningColorblind Safety



VT Maroon and VT Orange can appear identical under red-green colorblindness (~8% of men). When both appear on the same chart, differentiate with shape or line type in addition to color, or use scale_fill_vvn("accessible").



For palette functions, see the vvnthemes R Library →

title: Brand
text:
Typography

Fonts and Sizes










Style
Font
Size
Color
Use




Heading
Inter Bold
H1 32px · H2 24px · H3 18px
Maroon #861F41
Titles, section headings, tab labels


Body
Inter Regular
16px
Charcoal #3D3D3D
Paragraphs, axis labels, data labels


Caption
Inter Regular
14px
Gray #AAAAAA
Source lines, notes





Capitalization Rules








Element
Rule
Example




Chart title
Headline case
“Labor Force Participation Rate by Region”


Chart subtitle
Sentence case
“Virginia counties, 2018–2023”


Axis labels
Sentence case
“Percentage of households”


Map title
Headline case
“Median Household Income by County, Virginia”


Map subtitle
Sentence case
“2020 American Community Survey 5-year estimates”


Table title
Headline case
“Summary Statistics by County”


Column headings
Sentence case
“Poverty rate (%)”

title: Brand
text:
Logo Usage

Always use the official VT logo alongside VVN branding
Do not alter, recolor, or reposition the logo
Minimum clear space: equal to the height of the “V” in “Virginia” on all sides
The tagline “Invent the Future®” appears beneath the logo on branded materials

title: Tables
text:
VVN tables follow strict formatting rules for font styles, number alignment, and cell shading. These conventions make VVN tables look specific and consistent across all products. Every table must have a source line.

For R functions, gt styling, and implementation, see the Table Library →

title: Tables
text:
VVN tables follow strict formatting rules for font styles, number alignment, and cell shading. These conventions make VVN tables look specific and consistent across all products. Every table must have a source line.

For R functions, gt styling, and implementation, see the Table Library →

title: Tables
text:
Required elements









#
Element
Standard
Library function




1
Title
Headline case, bold, one line. Short and active. Example: “Virginia Counties at a Glance.”
vvn_table()


2
Subtitle
Sentence case (optional). States geography and year. Example: “Virginia, 2022 estimates.”
vvn_table()


3
Column headers
VT Maroon #861F41 fill, white bold text. Sentence case for standard columns; headline case for spanner headings.
vvn_table()


4
Alternating rows
Even rows use light gray #F7F7F7. No internal borders by default. No empty rows or columns.
vvn_table()


5
Cell alignment
Numbers: decimal-aligned, right. Text: left. Use consistent decimal places within each column.
vvn_table()


6
Source & notes
Source required below every table. Notes define abbreviations, methodology, and significance. Missing values: em dash (—), never blank.
vvn_table()

title: Tables
text:
Design standards
Number formatting



Value type
Format




Percentages
72.4% — one decimal place, percent sign


Dollar amounts
$48,200 — no decimals for whole dollars


Counts
1,234 — comma separator, no decimals


Index values
0.743 — three decimal places


P-values
< 0.001 or 0.023 — never 0.000



Column alignment

Numbers: right-aligned, decimal-point aligned
Text categories: left-aligned
Header text: centered over numeric columns, left-aligned over text columns

Missing data

Always use an em dash (—) for missing or suppressed values
Define suppression rules in the notes below the table
Never leave a cell blank

Spanner headers
Use spanner headings (headline case) to group related columns. Keep the spanner label short — 1–3 words.


For step-by-step R code and table examples, see the Table Library →
